description.value: An ancient tablet of carved bone, larger than a book, covered in Root-Runes, binding oaths, and records of flame-rites that predate written Svellheim history. The Bundingsteinn is the primary record of the binding mechanism by which a living Keeper becomes the Ever-Ember. Rickety Frets kept it for centuries as Svellheim’s most dangerous secret. It was stolen three weeks ago.
description.director:
ACQUISITION: Stolen from the Bioluminescent Library 3 weeks before the party arrives. Rickety Frets tasks them with finding it as part of his Three Demands oath. The Bundingsteinn was taken by a Wyrd-Draugr on behalf of Grafvitnir. The party may or may not recover it during Act 2; if it is recovered, it is returned to Rickety Frets or brought to the Ivory Tower.

THE THREE DEMANDS (Beat 12): Rickety Frets will not negotiate or help unless the party swears: (1) Remember him; (2) Send word back after; (3) Protect or destroy the Bundingsteinn if Grafvitnir cannot have it.

CONTENTS (Plot Device):

  • The binding mechanism: how Muspelheim fire is anchored to a living vessel (the Ever-Ember rite)
  • The First Kindling oath-text in full, with commentary by early Keepers
  • Records of the relationship between Muspelheim fire and Yggdrasil’s root network
  • Evidence that this rite has been performed before — and that those who performed it did not return from their posts

KAELEN’S REACTION (if she reads it): Begins Layer 2 narrative arc. She reads in silence for several minutes, then closes the tablet. Does not speak of what she read until much later.
